subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 28 Feb
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 28 Feb_vF.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Performance Dashboard (as of 26 Feb)

A/ Topline/P&L


Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Feb proj ADO 176k, 99% RR; ADO as %CB +0.23pp MoM; proj ADG
   $2.0M, 102%RR; ADG as %CB +0.32pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP proj ADO 99% RR, ADO as % CB +0.03pp MoM. Slight gap in RR
      mainly due to MY market platform performance lower than expected
      2.

      CNSIP proj ADO 112% RR, ADO as % CB +0.10pp MoM; above target driven
      by Price reduction initiative in PH and VN
      2.

   [P&L]
   1.

      Both Local SIP P+SA and CN SIP SA expect to land at full month Feb
      PC2 breakeven
      2.

      Overall combined PC2 healthy at Shopee level at $112k/day
      ($3.1m/month), +25% MoM
      1.

         PC2 MoM growth much higher than ADG growth (+3% MoM) due to 1)
         WHOB orders recovery from CNY; 2) P&L budget under-allocation
and marketing
         under-spending, mainly due to conservative budgeting approach
in Feb to
         ensure P&L breakeven
         2.

         From Mar, adopt new budgeting approach to better support marketing
         budget utilization and topline growth whereby 1) aggressively allocate
         budget in 1H 2) cut if necessary in 2H


B/ ADO breakdown (multiple BU/lanes)


Highlights

   1.

   IDMY proj 95% RR; Platform ADO% grew by 0.1p.p. Also overperforms CB
   with MoM increase in ADO %CB by 1.1pp.
   2.

   VNMY proj 84% RR; low RR due to slower Tet recovery that sellers are
   only fully back on 20 Feb, too late to recover ADO for the full-month; but
   L5D ADO already recovered to ~8K ADO range
   3.

   IDSG proj 107% RR; MoM increase in ADO% CB by 0.3pp with growth in
   fashion and lifestyle cluster with traffic growth; increased ads investment
   MoM leveraging on automation tool for new SKUs set up


C/ BWT


Highlights

   1.

   VNMY 90% RR, +1.7 days MoM: mainly driven by 2 days delay in KUL CC
   inspection and KCH CC. Has p

content.answer:
Hi Ian,


   -

   Can we always display P&L for the month? And we should probably have
   some targets? Instead of leaving it blank?
   -

      Noted will show P&L on monthly basis, attached below with dashboard
      MTD as of 26 Feb ($2.9M)
      -

      For SIP P&L, our OKR target is P MP PC1 + SIP standalone PC2
      breakeven, which we are hitting target since Jan
      -

      The combined PC2 on Shopee level is mainly the MP PC2 collected by
      the A markets
      -

         We did not set the target as it is a passive result of A market
         platform take rate minus PRM based on the topline we generate
in each A
         market
         -

         We do have monthly projection - we will include it from next update



   -

   IDMY should be growing MOM? Given that it is Raya season? Any reason why
   it is not the case?
   -

      The key reason is Raya trends this year kicked off much later than
      expected (25 Feb this year vs 15 Feb last year, 10 days late)
      -

      From 1-24 Feb, MY fashion cluster ADO saw -11.2% MoM. IDMY SIP Raya
      related categories (Muslim Fashion, Women Clothes, Men Clothes) performed
      slightly better than platform despite much lower platform-sponsored ads
      budget than last year, but overall impacted by low platform traffic.
      -

      From 25 Feb onwards, we saw significant growth in Raya categories. MY
      platform fashion cluster ADO +16.7% MoM, IDMY SIP fashion +45.8% MoM. The
      growth is Driven by
      -

         Platform level: Extra platform PRM has driven more Raya buyers to
         purchase on Shopee
         -

         For IDMY SIP specifically
         -

         Ramped up paid ads for new Raya SKUs
         -

         Sourced 10K trendy SKUs to push (Hijab, traditional wears, etc)
         -

         Revived 6 mil SKU into MY since 28 Feb


On Brunei

   -

   How are the deliveries being done ri

content.question: - IDMY should be growing MOM?
Given that it is Raya season?

Any reason
   why it is not the case?

- The key reason is Raya trends this year kicked off much later than
      expected (25 Feb this year vs 15 Feb last year, 10 days late)
      - From 1-24 Feb, MY fashion cluster ADO saw -11.2% MoM. IDMY SIP Raya
      related categories (Muslim Fashion, Women Clothes, Men Clothes) performed
      slightly better than platform despite much lower platform-sponsored ads
      budget than last year, but overall impacted by low platform traffic.
      - From 25 Feb onwards, we saw significant growth in Raya categories.
      MY platform fashion cluster ADO +16.7% MoM, IDMY SIP fashion +45.8% MoM.
      The growth is Driven by
         - Platform level: Extra platform PRM has driven more Raya buyers
         to purchase on Shopee
         - For IDMY SIP specifically
         - Ramped up paid ads for new Raya SKUs
         - Sourced 10K trendy SKUs to push (Hijab, traditional wears, etc)
         - Revived 6 mil SKU into MY since 28 Feb





How else can we grow IDMY more during this period?
- Do we need to teach them on how to handle delivery?

Or expect them to
   figure out themselves?

- We believe they can figure out themselves because
         - Runner services are popular in Brunei
         - Plenty of tutorial videos/advertisements on social media
         - Promoting runner service is risky due to legal concerns over
         their unofficial status



Are we sure about this?

*From:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Wednesday, 5 March 2025 10:28 am
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <
jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <
momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong
Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>;
Wei Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <
nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <
joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Johan Liew <johan.liew@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <
jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>; Jules
Zhao <jules.zhao@shopee.com>; Tina Tai <tina.tai@shopee.com>; Tzong Meng
Ong <tzongmeng.ong@shopee.com>; Mingkit Tan <mingkit.tan@shopee.com>; Lim
Wei <wei.lim@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 28 Feb



Hi Ian,



   - Can we always display P&L for the month?

And we should probably have
   some targets?
Instead of leaving it blank?

- Noted will show P&L on monthly basis, attached below with dashboard
      MTD as of 26 Feb ($2.9M)
      - For SIP P&L, our OKR target is P MP PC1 + SIP standalone PC2
      breakeven, which we are hitting target since Jan
      - The combined PC2 on Shopee level is mainly the MP PC2 collected by
      the A markets


   - We did not set the target as it is a passive result of A market
         platform take rate minus PRM based on the topline we generate
in each A
         market
         - We do have monthly projection - we will include it from next
         update



   - IDMY should be growing MOM?
Given that it is Raya season?

Any reason
   why it is not the case?

- The key reason is Raya trends this year kicked off much later than
      expected (25 Feb this year vs 15 Feb last year, 10 days late)
      - From 1-24 Feb, MY fashion cluster ADO saw -11.2% MoM. IDMY SIP Raya
      related categories (Muslim Fashion, Women Clothes, Men Clothes) performed
      slightly better than platform despite much lower platform-sponsored ads
      budget than last year, but overall impacted by low platform traffic.
      - From 25 Feb onwards, we saw significant growth in Raya categories.
      MY platform fashion cluster ADO +16.7% MoM, IDMY SIP fashion +45.8% MoM.
      The growth is Driven by


   - Platform level: Extra platform PRM has driven more Raya buyers to
         purchase on Shopee
         - For IDMY SIP specifically
         - Ramped up paid ads for new Raya SKUs
         - Sourced 10K trendy SKUs to push (Hijab, traditional wears, etc)
         - Revived 6 mil SKU into MY since 28 Feb



*On Brunei*

   - How are the deliveries being done right now?

How does the
   registration/downloads and buyers/downloads compare with new users in MY?

- Brunei buyers arrange their own runner service. Parcel will be
      delivered to a Malaysian address near Brunei via shopee MY logistics, and
      then the runner collects it and delivers it to Brunei within four days.
      - Pending MY BI provides new user conversion data for BN-MY
      comparison. ETA 7 Mar.
      - Also heads up that we will review the BN entry assessment in end
      Mar as planned, to see any changes on market potential, buyer sentiment,
      and logistics solutions. Will provide review update in April’s
SIP monthly
      meeting



   - Do we need to teach them on how to handle delivery?

Or expect them to
   figure out themselves?

- We believe they can figure out themselves because


   - Runner services are popular in Brunei
         - Plenty of tutorial videos/advertisements on social media
         - Promoting runner service is risky due to legal concerns over
         their unofficial status



*On counter TB*



Slide 6

   - Why so many sellers opt out?

- To clarify, 8 and 9 self-run sellers opted out from SG and MY
      respectively
      - Sellers opted out from SG/MY mainly because they want focus
      capacity on bigger markets like VN/TH, as they still believe SG/MY has
      limited growth potential
      - The ADO impact of these sellers to the program is ~7% for MY, and
      ~10% for SG



Slide 10

   - What is the expectation around growth for March?

- Mar target is 22% ADO growth for SG and 20% ADO growth for MY
      - Target to grow SKU count (+16k for SG, 14% MoM; +52k for MY, 480%
      MoM), with 1) local wishlist SKUs uploaded by sellers; 2) SKUs
migration of
      top GGP to MY; 3) onboarding newly acquired top TB sellers (not on SHP)
      - Maintain/Grow SKU efficiency in MY/SG by pushing the customised
      incentive package with top sellers, which includes sellers sourcing and
      investing in ads/vouchers, in exchange for RM support, comms waiver (SG
      only), PRM support, and other exposure points



   - For MY, how are we intending to show that we have more furniture SKUs
   now?

- MY will increase the marketing exposure to the furniture SKUs via 1)
      Shopee Home Microsite; 2) AMS; 3) SS slots



Slide 14

   - What are next steps then?
If it is not attractive?

content.answer:
Conduct 6% price reduction for top 500K non-performing SKUs in fashion
   categories, ranked by their ID local ADO. ETA tomorrow 7 Mar.
Ramp up AMS for top shops covering 20% of ADO. ETA today.

Continuously monitor and check SIP’s SKUs’ performance against platform
   on top Raya Keywords; to ramp up paid ads/rebates quickly to drive up
   traffic/conversion if PnL has surplus



   -

   Do we need to teach them on how to handle delivery? Or expect them to
   figure out themselves?
   -

      We believe they can figure out themselves because
      -

         Runner services are popular in Brunei
         -

         Plenty of tutorial videos/advertisements on social media
         -

         Promoting runner service is risky due to legal concerns over their
         unofficial status

Are we sure about this?



   -

   We discussed further with MY marketing - we are not able to identify any
   marketing moves that can enhance buyer’s awareness of runner service,
   without the risk of being traced back to Shopee
   -

   Overall there is limited room for us to navigate as we need to keep a
   safety space with “runner service” to mitigate any legal/GR risks
   -

   To help BN buyers in the long-run, we need to revisit direct shipping
   options. SIP SLS team will lead the reassessment and conclude by end-Mar

Hi Ian,

How else can we grow IDMY more during this period?

We have three additional WIP actions to grow IDMY more

   1.

   Conduct 6% price reduction for top 500K non-performing SKUs in fashion
   categories, ranked by their ID local ADO. ETA tomorrow 7 Mar.
   2.

   Ramp up AMS for top shops covering 20% of ADO. ETA today.
   3.

   Continuously monitor and check SIP’s SKUs’ performance against platform
   on top Raya Keywords; to ramp up paid ads/rebates quickly to drive u
